headline: JPMorgan’s US$8b EA Buyout Deal And What It Means For Valuation
summary: JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) is leading a consortium of banks in launching an $8b junk bond sale. The debt issuance is intended to finance the leveraged buyout of video game publisher Electronic Arts. The transaction highlights JPMorgan's central role in arranging large, higher risk financing for major deals. For you as an investor, this deal points to where JPMorgan's investment banking engine is currently focused: large, complex financings that rely on appetite for below investment grade...

headline: Stocks Rise, Oil Falls as Trump Eases Iran Threats: Markets Wrap
summary: (Bloomberg) -- A possible de-escalation in the Middle East conflict rippled through markets, with oil falling and stocks rising as President Donald Trump said he’d postpone strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure after what he described as productive talks toward ending hostilities.Brent dropped as much as 14% to $96, before paring the move and hovering near $102 as Iranian media reported there hasn’t been any communication with the US. headline: A Look At Electronic Arts (EA) Valuation As Shares Hover Near US$200 After Strong Multi Year Returns
summary: Electronic Arts stock in focus Electronic Arts (EA) is back on investor radars after recent trading left the share price near US$200, alongside mixed short term returns and stronger performance over the past year and multi year period. See our latest analysis for Electronic Arts. At around US$200.51, the recent share price pullback over the quarter contrasts with Electronic Arts's stronger one year and multi year total shareholder returns, which points to momentum that has cooled slightly...
